person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
John-1 and Tabitha (—?—) Thomas: End-of-Line Ancestors of Unknown Origins

New Haven, CT: Families of Ancient New Haven. (Online database. AmericanAncestors.org. New England Historic Genealogical Society, 2008.) Originally published as New Haven genealogical magazine. vols. I-VIII. Compiled by Donald Lines Jacobus. 8 vols. Rome, New York: Clarence D. Smith, 1923-1932.

http://www.americanancestors.org/DB98/i/7493/1727/0

===

THOMAS, John & Tabitha
Cliff McCarthy, 2016

After rampant theories and speculation, we really have no idea who John Thomas’ parents were. He was born around 1614. Both John and his wife Tabitha were presumably born in England. The exact date of their immigration is unknown but certainly occurred sometime before 1640, during the great influx of English people to New England. They settled in New Haven Colony where they may have married.

There is a John Thomas who at age 14 emigrated from England to America aboard the Hopewell in September of 1635. It is possible that this is our John Thomas, but no one knows for sure.

John Thomas married Tabitha (surname unknown) about 1639. It is not known whether they married in England or at New Haven, CT where they settled to raise their 7 children. It is also not known whether their first 3 children were born in England or at New Haven, CT.
John Thomas did not sign the Fundamental Agreement that the founders of New Haven, CT signed on 4 June 1639.  However, John and Tabitha were definitely in New Haven by 1646 when they testified in court about a case of sexual harassment. They were both assigned seats in the meetinghouse in early 1647. Their fourth child, Elizabeth, born in 1648, appears in the baptismal records of the New Haven church, yet she wasn’t baptized until 1653. John Thomas appears on the 1669 Freeman list for New Haven.

His will was dated 4 January 1669/70 at New Haven and his estate inventory of  9 (or 19) January 1672 was for 174 pounds 6 shillings 6 pence. His will named wife Tabitha; sons Daniel and John and Samuel and Joseph; daughters Elizabeth and Tabitha; and grandchildren by daughter Sarah Wilmot. Tabitha Thomas’s will was dated 22 March 1690 and it named son Samuel Thomas as executor.

https://commonheroes3.wordpress.com/11th-generation/thomas-john-tabitha/

zh.lifeSummary: John Thomas於大約1616年出生在英國。他於1640年與Tabitha在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1669年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文。他在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 哈文劍橋大學默里 哈文被列為proprietor。他去世於1671年12月15日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂享年56歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州劍橋大學默里 哈文劍橋大學默里 哈文特 邱奇 奧 馮 格林 亞德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Thomas于大约1616年出生在英国。他于1640年与Tabitha在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1669年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文。他在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文劍橋大學默里 哈文被列为proprietor。他去世于1671年12月15日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂享年56岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文劍橋大學默里 哈文特 邱奇 奧 馮 格林 亞德。

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
"Dutch explorer Abel Tasman discovers part of the western littoral of New Zealand and names his discovery ""Staten Landt""."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.
New Haven County was created on 10 May 1666, and was named for an English port town called Newhaven, London, England

en.lifeSummary: John Thomas was born about 1616, in England. He married Tabitha in 1640, in British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He lived in New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America in 1669. His occupation is listed as proprietor in New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, United States. He died on 15 December 1671, in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 56, and was buried in Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: John Thomas est né environ 1616, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Tabitha en 1640, à Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il a vécu à New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1669. Sa profession de son indiquée est proprietor à New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, États-Unis. Il est décédé le 15 décembre 1671, à Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: John Thomas wurde um 1616 in England geboren. Er hat 1640 in British Colonial America Tabitha geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1669 hat er in New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. sein Beruf ist als proprietor in New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten aufgeführt. Er starb am 15. Dezember 1671 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: John Thomas è nato il circa 1616 nel England. Lui sposato Tabitha nel 1640, nel British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Vissuto a New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America nel 1669. La sua occupazione è indicata come proprietor nel New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America. Morì il 15 dicembre 1671, nel Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: John Thomasは約1616年にイギリスで生まれた。1640年にイギリス植民地アメリカでTabithaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1669年にはイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンに住んでいた。アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニュー ヘイベンでの職業はproprietorと記録されている。彼は1671年12月15日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニューヘイベン トウンで56歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ニュー ヘイベンニュー ヘイベンセンター チャーチ オン ス グレン チャーチヤルドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Thomas(은)는 대략 1616년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1640년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아에서 Tabitha(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1669년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴 하븐에서 proprietor이었다. 그는 1671년 12월 15일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴 하븐 세터 c후르치  테 그린 c후르c히아어드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: John Thomas nasceu aproximadamente 1616, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Tabitha em 1640, em British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele viveu em New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America em 1669. Sua profissão aparece como proprietor em New Haven, New Haven, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América. Ele faleceu em 15 de dezembro de 1671, em Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Center Church On The Green Churchyard, New Haven, New Haven, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: John Thomas родился около 1616 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Tabitha в 1640 г. в местности бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он жил в местности ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1669 году.  Он работал по профессии proprietor в местности ню хавен, ню хавен, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты. Он умер 15 декабря 1671 г. в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности кентир чурч он т грин чурчярд, ню хавен, ню хавен, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

Vivió en New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America en 1669. Su ocupación registrada era proprietor en New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
